titles.title: And Can It Be, That I Should Gain

authors.author: Charles Wesley
Thomas Campbell
Ist's wirklich wahr, dass mir zugut der Heiland starb

properties.verseOrder: v1 v2 v3 v2 v4 v2 v5 v2 v6 v2

verse.name: v1
verse.lines: And can it be that I should gain An int'rest in the Savior's blood? Died He for me, who caused His pain? For me, who Him to death pursued? Amazing love! how can it be That Thou, my God, should die for me?

verse.name: v2
verse.lines: Amazing love! how can it be That Thou, my God, should die for me!

verse.name: v3
verse.lines: 'Tis mystery all! Th'Immortal dies! Who can explore His strange design? In vain the firstborn seraph tries To sound the depths of love divine! 'Tis mercy all! let earth adore, Let angel minds inquire no more.

verse.name: v4
v5
v6
verse.lines: No condemnation now I dread; Jesus, and all in Him is mine! Alive in Him, my living Head, And clothed in righteousness divine, Bold I approach th'eternal throne, And claim the crown, through Christ my own.
